title: salix obtusissima
intro: Salix obtusissima, also known as the roughleaf willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can be used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The salicylic acid found in Salix obtusissima provides relief from pain and reduces inflammation, offering health benefits such as alleviating headaches and improving digestion. Herbal preparations of Salix obtusissima include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make salves and ointments for topical application.
